markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Verify a context bundle can be reconstructed from QueryPlan, RetrievalTrace, sources, spans, and EventLedger events. This MT belongs to SecurityPortabilityValidation. Prove WP-009 behavior with negative fixtures, visual debugging, portability checks, and validator-ready evidence.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Validation must include negative fixtures for false PASS, stale state, missing Postgres, missing assets, secret leakage, partial PDF extraction, parallel write conflicts, and UI overlap.
Portability checks must reject hardcoded machine paths and authority stored only in generated projections.
scope.expected_behavior: RetrievalTraceReplayFixture leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: RetrievalTraceReplayFixture is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence.summary: MT-238 now replays persisted RetrievalTrace -> ContextBundle rows from durable PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority with fail-closed receipt validation, selected-vs-included validation, required selected candidate rows, workspace/run/aggregate checks, and evidence reconstruction for span/source/passage/claim/entity plus executor graph entity_ref rows whose ref_id is a knowledge_edges.relationship_id. ContextBundleCompilerV2 now records candidate rows for admitted compiler candidates when the executor did not already provide them, while avoiding duplicate candidates.

commands.result: PASS; 7/7 real PostgreSQL replay tests passed, 0 failed, 0 ignored, 0 filtered. Covers receipt validation, selected-item mismatch, missing selected candidate rejection, executor passage anchors, and executor graph-edge relationship_id replay.

evidence.summary: Re-review found no blocking findings. It verified the previous selected-candidate validation and PostgreSQL false-green blockers were fixed, confirmed compiler candidate recording/deduplication, and confirmed executor graph-edge replay resolves relationship_id through knowledge_edges plus knowledge_edge_spans rather than fake entity lookup. Residual non-blocking concern: default parallel test execution may be environmentally flaky on cold managed-PostgreSQL startup; controlled proof used the focused real-PostgreSQL target.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-238 (RetrievalTraceReplayFixture) requires proving a ContextBundle can be reconstructed from a persisted RetrievalTrace, QueryPlan, sources, spans, and EventLedger receipts on durable PostgreSQL authority, with negative fixtures. The code delivers a real, fully-implemented replay function plus a 7-test suite against Handshake-managed real PostgreSQL with fail-closed negative cases; it aligns with current Master Spec 2.3.13.11.
findings: storage/knowledge_retrieval.rs:177-325 replay_context_bundle_from_trace is a real implementation: loads the durable knowledge_retrieval_traces row (get_trace_by_id, raw SQL :327-355), the bundle+items, deserializes typed QueryPlan/RetrievalTrace from decisions JSONB, validates query_plan_id linkage and bundle.allowed_context query_plan_id (:215-224), enforces selected==included set equality and candidate-kind matching (:226-274), reconstructs span/source/passage/entity/claim evidence from committed rows (:357-574), and loads two EventLedger receipts with strict event_type/aggregate_type/aggregate_id/run-context validation (get_receipt_event :587-646). Fails closed (StorageError::Validation) on every mismatch.

Evidence reconstruction is genuinely durable-backed: replay_span_evidence (:401) reads knowledge_spans + knowledge_sources; replay_entity_evidence (:481) resolves graph-edge relationship_id via get_knowledge_edge_by_relationship_id then list_knowledge_edge_span_ids (real knowledge_edges/knowledge_edge_spans), not a fake entity lookup — confirms the evidence summary's graph-edge claim.

tests/knowledge_retrieval_replay_tests.rs (538 lines, 7 #[tokio::test]) run against Handshake-managed real PostgreSQL: knowledge_memory_fixtures.rs + knowledge_pg_support.rs use ManagedPostgres::ensure_running (port 5544, data dir under Handshake_Artifacts) and db.run_migrations(); header states 'Real PostgreSQL only. No SQLite, no mocks.' skip_if_no_pg! (:36-48) PANICS if PG absent — no silent green.

Negative fixtures are real and corrupt actual rows via sqlx on a live pool: wrong receipt event_type (:217), trace/bundle workspace drift (:237), missing build_receipt_event_id (:260) and trace_receipt_event_id (:278), aggregate_id mismatch (:311), empty selected (:333), missing ranked candidate (:361) — each asserts expect_err with a specific message. Covers the MT's required negative cases (false PASS, stale state, missing receipts, parallel-write/workspace conflicts).

compiler.rs:250-282 records RetrievalCandidate rows for admitted candidates when the executor did not already provide them, with .any() dedup guard (:256-258) — matches the evidence summary fixing the prior selected-candidate false-green.

No placeholders/escapes: grep for todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/Unavailable/'not yet wired'/'pending MT-' across the three files returned zero hits. No forbidden deps (sqlite/elasticsearch/opensearch/meilisearch/typesense/docker/redis/valkey) present.

Current Master Spec v02.194 section 2.3.13.11 (02-system-architecture.md:2694-2706) requires RetrievalTrace as 'a replayable record' on PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority — the MT proof exercises exactly this.

Non-blocking paperwork aside: lifecycle.implementation_commit is null; work is folded into broad checkpoint commit c49afb7f rather than an MT-238-named commit. Expected during governance wind-down; not a failing reason.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
